(9 comments)

Patch Set 2:

(7 comments)

Patch Set 2:

The good news is that this patch reduces the runtime of sanity-lfsck and sanity-scrub by as much as 3500s, so it has made a significant difference. 

The sanity-scrub test is still taking quite a long time (5500s) even after this patch, so it might be useful to look into the test results to see where the test time is taken to try and further optimize those tests while still getting the same test coverage.

Patch Set 2:

The slow sanity-scrub is because bad performance of createmany on Maloo, I do not know why it is so slow, less than 50 objects/sec, no detailed logs.

Patch Set 2:

It my local test, the run time is reduced to about 30%  or less. But the Maloo does not show the same good result.

Patch Set 13: Code-Review+2

It would be great if these patches were not all in a single series. In this case it is not possible to land these patches out of order, and if any patch needs to be refreshed then all of the later patches may also need to be refreshed. It is better to keep  each patch on a separate branch unless they are actually dependent on each other.

parents.subject: LU-4895 lfsck: not create object to repair dangling by default

commit.subject: LU-4556 tests: speed up sanity-lfsck and sanity-scrub tests
commit.message:
LU-4556 tests: speed up sanity-lfsck and sanity-scrub tests

1) drop unnecessary devices reformat.
2) drop unnecessary system stop/re-start.
3) replace 'sleep' with wait_update_facet to avoid idle wait.
4) drop unnecessary "-p" option for some "mkdir" cases.
5) replace "touch" with "createmany -m".
6) other code style changes and cleanup.

Test-Parameters: envdefinitions=SLOW=yes testlist=lfsck-performance
Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I047c63b8793fa843fabe1a69b332c42f2f523f68
